title: sapindus edulis
intro: Sapindus edulis, also known as the soapbark tree, is a deciduous tree native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, characterized by its smooth, gray bark and small yellow flowers. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of saponins. The health benefits of Sapindus edulis include its ability to treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and improve digestion. Herbal preparations of Sapindus edulis include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
